mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.5%, it signals a deteriorating labor market that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate profit growth, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and consumer price indices indicate robust demand pulling through inflation, which confirms strong corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in greater than 60% of cases, or the interaction term loses predictive power when tested against single-series baselines.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production signals improving supply-side capacity utilization and manufacturing output, which triggers short-term positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days during economic recovery phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO expansions fail to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery periods, or the mechanism shows negative predictive accuracy in greater than 50% of recovery windows.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product reflects sustained aggregate demand expansion, which supports corporate earnings trajectories and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows no correlation with 60-day forward returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 330.0, it indicates entrenched inflation that forces monetary tightening expectations, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in greater than 65% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 330.0, or the threshold effect disappears in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirm a tightening labor market that boosts wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative forward returns in greater than 55% of observations, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts signal weakening residential investment and reduced construction activity, which drags on economic momentum and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days during late-cycle phases.
failure_condition: HOUST declines fail to predict negative 5-day returns during late-cycle periods, or the signal shows positive predictive accuracy in greater than 60% of tested cases.
